prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
It's a new day, and you've got this. That task you've been putting off? It's not some monumental mountain—it's a series of small, totally manageable steps, and you have all the tools you need to tackle each one.

Think of it this way: every moment you've procrastinated is a minute that future you will thank present you for getting started. It's normal to feel a little intimidated. That means you're about to do something important, something that matters. But feeling intimidated doesn't change the fact that you are capable.

Just do the first thing. Read the first email. Open the first document. Take that first step. Once you start, momentum builds. Time flies when you're focused on the little things one after another.

You're stronger than your hesitation. This task is smaller than your courage. There's no better time than right this second. Go get it done—you've got this.
